purpose: In-process Node.js debugger via the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). Spawns a child Node process with `--inspect-brk=0` (random port, break on first line), parses the `Debugger listening on ws://...` URL from stderr, opens a WebSocket using Node 22+ built-in global WebSocket, enables Debugger + Runtime domains, calls `Runtime.runIfWaitingForDebugger`, and exposes a high-level API to the renderer (setBreakpoint, removeBreakpoint, resume, pause, stepOver/Into/Out, evaluate, getVariables). Translates CDP events (Debugger.paused, Runtime.consoleAPICalled, Runtime.exceptionThrown) into renderer push events (debug-paused, debug-output, debug-resumed, debug-stopped). Single-session: only one debug process can run at a time.
gotchas: Requires Node.js 22+ for the global WebSocket API. Older Electron versions bundling Node <22 will fail at `new WebSocket(url)`.
ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE is deleted before spawn — without this the child runs as Electron and `--inspect-brk` is ignored, leaving the debugger waiting forever.
Breakpoint URLs use `file://` + `fs.realpathSync(filePath)` to match V8's internal URL form (e.g., /tmp -> /private/tmp on macOS). Skipping realpath causes 'breakpoint not bound' silently.
CDP line/column numbers are 0-based; this service converts to 1-based at every boundary. Mixing the two will misplace UI breakpoint markers.
Single-session enforced via the module-level `activeSession` — calling launch() while a session exists returns an error rather than queueing.

cleanupSession sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL after 2s, but if the WebSocket is already dropped (debuggee crashed) the close handler also fires cleanupSession — guards via `activeSession.ws === ws` check prevent double-cleanup.

sendCDPCommand uses `++activeSession.nextId` for correlation; if a response arrives AFTER its handler is removed (timeout fires first) the response is silently dropped. Long-running CDP calls (>10s default) will fail.
`scriptSources` map is populated only via Debugger.scriptParsed events — frames from before the WS was attached have no scriptId mapping (rare since we break on first line).
patterns: Stderr-scrape pattern: parse `Debugger listening on (ws://...)` regex from cumulative stderr buffer to find the CDP endpoint.
Promise-bridged CDP commands: every sendCDPCommand attaches a one-shot `message` listener that filters by `id`, with timeout cleanup.

Renderer push events use 4 channels: `debug-paused`, `debug-resumed`, `debug-output`, `debug-stopped`. The paused payload includes callStack, scopes, pausedFile, pausedLine, hitBreakpoints — enough for a full IDE pause UI in one frame.
Two evaluate paths: Debugger.evaluateOnCallFrame (paused state, with frame context) vs Runtime.evaluate (global runtime); same return shape via an inline RemoteObject -> simplified-shape mapper.
conventions: All async functions return `{success, ...}` or `{success: false, error}` — consistent with other services.
Console-log every lifecycle transition with `[DebugService]` prefix.
1-based lines/columns are the contract on the public API; 0-based only inside CDP boundaries.
